response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a seasoned full-stack web developer with 20+ years of experience, but is fundamentally mismatched for this role on multiple hard requirements. Most critically, native-level Japanese proficiency is listed as a mandatory qualification and the candidate has no Japanese language skills whatsoever. Beyond the language barrier, the candidate's expertise is concentrated in TypeScript, Node.js, and web/cloud development — with negligible evidence of the C programming, Linux kernel development, embedded firmware, or network security domain knowledge (MACsec, 802.1X, L2/L3 switching) that this role demands.
strengths: Over 20 years of professional software development experience spanning diverse environments including some embedded and hardware-adjacent work
Demonstrated C/C++ exposure at National Instruments (LabWindows/CVI) and in personal projects (Sound Position Server)
Strong Linux background and DevOps familiarity (Docker, CI/CD, GitLab administration)
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency at any level — this is a stated hard requirement for the role
No experience in network security technologies required by the role: MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal, SNMP, NETCONF, or L2/L3 switch development
Primary technical stack (TypeScript, Node.js, React, MongoDB) is entirely web/cloud-oriented with no embedded firmware or kernel development experience
